step_by_step_thinking: Common signs of occlusal trauma (TFO) involve changes in tooth stability and alignment. Among the given options, tooth mobility stands out as a direct indicator of altered positioning and potential instability caused by trauma. While cusp fractures might accompany TFO, they are secondary findings rather than the primary hallmark. Similarly, resorption could eventually manifest post-injury but isn't immediate or common enough to serve as a frequent identifier right away. Lastly, widening of periodontal ligaments generally signifies inflammation or disease processes beyond typical trauma-induced disturbances.
raw_response:
Common signs of occlusal trauma (TFO) involve changes in tooth stability and alignment. Among the given options, tooth mobility stands out as a direct indicator of altered positioning and potential instability caused by trauma. While cusp fractures might accompany TFO, they are secondary findings rather than the primary hallmark. Similarly, resorption could eventually manifest post-injury but isn't immediate or common enough to serve as a frequent identifier right away. Lastly, widening of periodontal ligaments generally signifies inflammation or disease processes beyond typical trauma-induced disturbances.
\boxed{A}
